title: Xyloid Aerated Egyptian Wagyu Kebabs
description: A unique and flavorful Egyptian dish featuring wagyu beef, kombu cold extract, and a yeast-based aerating agent, perfect for special occasions. This dairy-free friendly recipe is sure to please crowds.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together lemon juice, olive oil, garlic, ginger, salt, and black pepper.
Add the wagyu beef cubes to the bowl and marinate for at least 30 minutes.
In a small bowl, mix together kombu cold extract and yeast.
Add the kombu-yeast mixture to the marinated beef and mix well.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and let it sit at room temperature for 1 hour to allow the yeast to aerate the mixture.
Preheat a grill or grill pan to medium-high heat.
Thread the marinated beef cubes onto skewers, leaving a small space between each cube.
Grill the kebabs for 8-10 minutes per side, or until they reach an internal temperature of 130 degrees.
Let the kebabs rest for 5 minutes before serving.
Serve the kebabs hot, garnished with fresh parsley or cilantro if desired.
